Summary of the Peer Review Assessment for the Pre-load
Air Cargo Targeting Program
Transport Canada’s Pre-load Air Cargo Targeting (PACT) Program developed a system to
identify potential security risks in air cargo bound for Canada. The system uses a risk
algorithm to assess cargo shipment data. The system automatically assesses shipments
with pre-defined, low-risk traits. Shipments that are deemed to be higher risk are sent to
humans for review.
Why we did a peer review assessment
The PACT program did an Algorithmic Impact Assessment, and scored 2 out 4 for impact
level. Because of this, the program was required to do a peer review of the system’s use of
AI.
Following the rules in the Treasury Board Secretariat Directive on Automated Decision-
Making, Shared Services Canada’s (SSC) Artificial Intelligence Centre of Excellence (AICoE)
conducted an independent peer review of the PACT system. The peer review assessment
serves as a quality assurance mechanism ensuring decision-making systems are assessed
by qualified experts for integrity, ethical considerations, and technical soundness.
Summary and recommendations
Throughout the assessment process, the AICoE engaged with numerous PACT subject
matter experts, reviewed provided artefacts and received a demonstration of the PACT
system. Artefacts covered how the system works, why some tasks are automated, how
risks are managed, and how the system and algorithm are designed.
Here’s what the peer review found:
• The impact score of 2 in the Algorithmic Impact Assessment (AIA) was accurate. The
possible effects of the algorithm were correctly identified by the team
o They still recommended updating the AIA to make it clear that the 6%
number is just an estimate of how many shipments were manually checked
before the system was built
• PACT is appropriately managing the risks of using the algorithm:
o The team regularly checks and adjusts the system’s risk thresholds. This
helps avoid system mistakes, like labelling a high-risk shipment as low-risk
UUnnccllaassssiififieedd || NNoonn ccllaassssiififiéé
o PACT meets the rules in the directive, including giving staff proper training,
checking that the system works as expected, and making sure decisions
made by the system can be explained and tracked
• PACT regularly tests for data quality and has rules in place for handling data so that
it’s not kept for longer than it needs to be
• PACT has taken steps to make sure the system is fair. They held meetings with
stakeholders and tested the system to find and reduce bias. Some bias may still
happen during normal operations, but review processes help keep negative impacts
low
• PACT has a clear decision-making process and keeps track of system and data
updates. There are no plans to shut down the system
• PACT has strong privacy protections in place and a privacy impact assessment has
been completed
• PACT follows departmental rules to manage security risks. Earlier independent
reviews also found that PACT does a good job of reducing air cargo security risks
• PACT has also prepared training materials and testing plans to make sure the
system works well and staff are ready to use it
Based on the provided artefacts, the AICoE found that PACT has taken the right steps to
make sure the system works properly and safely. PACT will keep improving and adjusting
the system and the work done by human analysts to meet the program’s goals.
Contact us
For more information about this peer review assessment please contact PACT-Information-
CFAPC@tc.gc.ca.
